title: Comprehension + adoption analytics (aggregate, de-identified)
context: For long-term sustainability and evidence-based iteration, open-pictograms needs a lightweight system to aggregate comprehension scores across symbols and cohorts over time, and to track downstream adoption (which partners and tools are using which symbols in which versions). Both datasets must be aggregate and de-identified — individual participant data is never stored or published. This analytics layer turns per-symbol comprehension scores into a cross-set quality dashboard and makes deployment impact visible.
objective: Design and implement a lightweight aggregate analytics dataset for comprehension scores and a deployment-adoption registry, both de-identified, queryable, and integrated with the CI pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Comprehension analytics dataset aggregates comprehensionScore records across all symbols: summary statistics (mean correct%, % meeting threshold, % requiring redesign-and-retest), breakdown by category and safety class, and longitudinal view across releases.

Adoption analytics dataset records downstream use: which symbol versions are deployed in which partner contexts, tool/format (OBF, SVG, PNG, signage sheet), and date — updated by the Steward as new deployments are confirmed.
Both datasets are aggregate and de-identified: no individual participant names, session IDs, or identifying context; comply with the consent protocol from writing-001.
Datasets are machine-readable (JSON or CSV) and published in analytics/ with clear data dictionaries; updated by the CI pipeline on each release for the comprehension aggregates.
Published in the repo and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
output: analytics/comprehension-summary.json (aggregate comprehension analytics) and analytics/adoption-registry.json (deployment adoption records); licensed CC-BY-4.0. PR opened against main.
